mismatches.issue: state override set to 0 bypasses environment MIN_MCAP_USD floor and allows entries below intended market cap threshold
state override lowers liquidity floor well below .env MIN_LIQUIDITY_FLOOR_USD

auto_corrections.reason: state override contradicted .env and would bypass mcap guard; safe to align to .env default
state override lowered liquidity floor; aligned to .env default

git_commit.reason: state directory is git-ignored in repo; changes were written to disk but not committed

ws_manager_enrollment.notes: Code binds BirdEye WS to wsmgr and calls wsmgr.onFill for each open position on startup. Current state shows 11 open positions; manager will subscribe them when runtime starts or on next boot.

lowSolPause_behavior.notes: Tracker/index toggles state.flags.lowSolPauseEntries true when on-chain SOL balance < MIN_SOL_FOR_FEES; state currently has it set true (new entries paused).

recommendations: Ensure state directory is tracked or persist filterOverrides in a tracked config file if you want configuration changes to be version-controlled.
If the filterOverrides were intentionally set to permissive values, review and document that exception. Otherwise keep the state overrides aligned to .env as applied here.
Monitor SOL balance and, if lowSolPauseEntries is unexpected, inspect recent balance history (state.track.lowSolAlertAtMs) and refill SOL reserve to resume entries.
Restart runtime (or trigger cache.set birdeye:sub:<mint>) to ensure WS manager subscribes current open positions if not already active.
